key_insight: ToMap improves proof autoformalization by spending test-time compute where it matters most: refining the decomposition of a proof into atomic, self-contained steps.
engineering_relevance: For theorem-proving systems, this suggests a practical inference-time strategy that boosts reliability without expensive retraining by optimizing the pipeline’s hardest bottleneck.
method: Bottleneck analysis identifies decomposition quality as the main failure source
GEPA-inspired prompt evolution uses verification progress and semantic rubrics to guide refinement
evidence_signal: On ProofFlowBench, ToMap outperforms the prior best method by 19.0% on both syntactic correctness and semantic faithfulness while using lower test-time cost.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of decomposition updates, so poorly designed prompts or limited verification signals could reduce gains or waste test-time budget.
paper_title: Efficient Test-Time Optimization for Multi-Agent Proof Autoformalization
